senses.gloss: to know, to have knowledge, to be cognizant

examples.text: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ لَا تُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ قَالُوا إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُصْلِحُونَ ۝ أَلَا إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ الْمُفْسِدُونَ وَلَٰكِنْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ

senses.gloss: to realize, to notice, to come to know
to perceive, to feel, to sense
to be conscious, to be aware

examples.english: He mingled with the poets and became a poet

senses.gloss: to perceive, to feel, to sense

examples.text: لَقَدْ كُنْتُ أَزْرَعُ شَعْرَ جَسْدِي مُنْذُ بُضِعِ سَنَوَاتٍ حَتَّى الْآنَ.
examples.english: I've been growing my hair for a few years now.

senses.gloss: to have thick and long hair, to be hirsute

etymology.text:
From Proto-Semitic *śaʕr- (“hair”).
Cognate with Hebrew שיער (se'ár).

Compare Hebrew שיר (shir) and Hebrew שירה (shirá).
